project.title: HaCaT ACSL4mRNA probe pull-down MS
project.projectDescription: ACSL4mRNA probe was designed for pull-down experiments in HaCaT lysates
project.sampleProcessingProtocol: Biotinylated random and ACSL4 RNA probes were designed and synthesized by RiboBio. For pull-down assays, 10^7 HaCaT cells were collected. Biotinylated random and ACSL4 RNA probes were added to the pretreated streptavidin magnetic beads (#HY-K0202, MedChemExpress, USA). After 1 h of co-incubation at room temperature, the probe-coated beads complex was added with protein lysate to prepare an RNA-Protein complex at 4℃ overnight. The next day, 50 μL Elution Buffer was used to elute the RNA-Protein complex from beads at 37℃ for 30 min. Eluted proteins were subjected to western blotting and mass spectrometry.
project.dataProcessingProtocol: Through steps such as noise removal, baseline correction, peak identification, etc., the signal-to-noise ratio and accuracy of the data are improved. Then, appropriate algorithms and tools were selected to effectively extract the key mass spectral peak information from the raw data. The mass spectral peaks obtained from the experiment were matched and aligned with known protein databases, and the data was deconvoluted using software tools to accurately determine the mass of the protein/peptide. The abundance and characteristics of the protein are then inferred by analyzing intensity, peptide count, and coverage.

references.referenceLine: Wu L, Li L, Wang W, Chen S, Saw PE, Hong X, Liang D, Yang C, Yan L, Lin WJ, Ren M. YTHDF2 regulates ACSL4-dependent ferroptosis of keratinocytes in diabetic wound healing. Clin Sci (Lond). 2025 139(16):CS20255877
